The community's quick response highlights the collaborative spirit among Heywood residents, with many willing to lend a hand in times of need. Those with similar concerns about structural issues in their homes are encouraged to seek professional advice to ensure safety and stability.

Bury North MP James Frith has raised concerns over Manchester Airport's new online parking system, which he claims is leading to unfair fines for local residents making short pick-up or drop-off visits. Frith highlighted that poor signage and a confusing online payment process are contributing factors to the issue, with many families reporting unexpected charges.

content_html:
Littleborough, located in the foothills of the south Pennines, is a charming town within the borough of Rochdale. Originally established as an Urban District in 1894, Littleborough became part of Rochdale in 1974.

The town is renowned for its natural beauty, with Hollingworth Lake serving as a major tourist attraction. The lake offers a variety of water sports, including rowing, sailing, and windsurfing, drawing visitors from across the region.

Littleborough's town centre is distinguished by its stone-built architecture and is designated as a conservation area, reflecting its historical significance and special architectural qualities. The conservation status helps preserve the town's unique character and heritage, making it a pleasant destination for both residents and visitors alike.

With its scenic surroundings and rich history, Littleborough continues to be a vibrant part of the Rochdale borough, appealing to nature enthusiasts and history buffs.

title: **Heywood: A Historical Mill Town with Unique Heritage**
excerpt: Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history rooted in the industrial revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000, the town is known for its nickname 'Monkey Town,'
content_html:
Heywood, a mill town located in Greater Manchester, has a rich history rooted in the industrial revolution. With a population of approximately 30,000, the town is known for its nickname 'Monkey Town,' which is believed to have originated from the local pronunciation of Heap Bridge, formerly referred to as 'ape bridge.'

In 1974, Heywood became part of the Rochdale borough, further integrating its historical significance into the region. The town's namesake, the Heywood family, played pivotal roles in British history. Notably, Peter Heywood was instrumental in foiling the Gunpowder Plot and apprehending Guy Fawkes. Another member of the family, also named Peter Heywood, gained notoriety in 1789 as a mutineer aboard HMS Bounty.

Heywood's industrial legacy and historical connections continue to shape its identity, making it a notable part of the Rochdale area. The town's blend of historical events and local culture contributes to its unique character within Greater Manchester.

title: **Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester: A New Heritage Tour Concept**
excerpt: Greater Manchester boasts a wealth of history, natural beauty, and unique architecture, yet lacks a comprehensive heritage tour across the region. In response, a proposal has emerged to create a ‘Seve
content_html:
Greater Manchester boasts a wealth of history, natural beauty, and unique architecture, yet lacks a comprehensive heritage tour across the region. In response, a proposal has emerged to create a ‘Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester’ tour, blending intriguing landmarks with cultural significance.

One notable candidate is the Stockport Pyramid, a ziggurat-style structure that has stood since 1992. Originally part of a planned series of pyramid-shaped buildings along the River Mersey, it served as a Co-op store until its closure at the end of the last decade. Despite efforts to revitalise the site, it currently remains unoccupied, serving as a prominent landmark on the M60.

Another contender for the list is the Four Pillars of the Rochdale Canal, located between Albion Lock and Tib Lock. These four columns, with three partially collapsed and overgrown, are remnants of warehouses that once lined the canal. While not the most visually striking feature of the area, they hold a sense of mystery and history, prompting local interest.

Residents are encouraged to share their suggestions for the Seven Wonders of Greater Manchester, as the concept aims to celebrate the region's rich heritage and unique landmarks.

Visitors will have the opportunity to witness the creation of these impressive murals in real time, as renowned artists from around the globe come together to showcase their talents. The murals will be situated within a 30-minute walking radius, forming a unique open-air gallery throughout the town. A map will be provided to help guide attendees to each artwork.

Among the participating artists are Insane51, Onur, Smug1, SecaOne, AYLO, Epod, and Lei Mai, all celebrated for their impactful contributions to street art. Lead artist and curator, Hayley Garner, known as AYLO, expressed her excitement about bringing her craft to her hometown during Rochdale's year as Greater Manchester Town of Culture.

AYLO, a prominent street artist and part of the acclaimed duo Nomad Clan, has gained international recognition for her large-scale murals, including the UK's tallest mural near Leeds train station. The work spans from Belfield to Gale and is part of the operator's proactive investment programme. Paul Riley, the project manager for the Rochdale area, emphasised the importance of this upgrade, stating, “This upgrade is a vital step in modernising our network and supporting the region’s future energy needs.” He noted that the replacement of ageing oil-filled cables with innovative technology will not only enhance reliability but also mitigate environmental risks.

SP Electricity North West is collaborating closely with Rochdale Council to ensure minimal disruption for businesses and residents during the works. Temporary traffic lights will be implemented on Park Road, Bond Street, and Yorkshire Road to facilitate safe operations. Customers have been informed of the project through hand-delivered letters.

Work has commenced on Park Road, progressing to March Street, and will subsequently move to Belfield Road, Whitworth Road, and Gale Street. The project is expected to benefit thousands of customers across the area.

Galloway, a well-known left-wing figure, is focusing his campaign on Rochdale's Muslim electorate, which constitutes around 30% of voters. He has been vocal about the ongoing situation in Gaza, stating, “The people of Gaza don't have a vote in this election, you do,” and promising to "shake the walls for Gaza" if elected.

Labour's candidate, Azhar Ali, has faced backlash after a recording of him making allegedly antisemitic remarks surfaced, leading to the party disowning him. Despite this, Ali remains on the ballot due to legal timing constraints. If elected, he would serve as an independent MP.

Voter sentiment appears mixed; while some Labour supporters are aware of the controversy, others remain committed to voting for the party. Local resident Paul Walsh, 56, expressed his intention to support Labour despite the ongoing issues, highlighting the complexity of the election landscape in Rochdale.
